description:
Job Requirements

Role/ Job Title: State Head - Gold Loan

Function/ Department: Gold Loan

Job Purpose

The State Head – Gold Loan is responsible for driving gold loan business growth across multiple channels within the assigned state. This includes identifying high-potential locations, expanding distribution networks beyond branch banking, and leading a team of dedicated sales professionals. The role demands strategic planning, operational excellence, and a strong focus on customer experience and portfolio quality.

Identify branches and potential locations for gold loan business through market surveys and competitive analysis of NBFCs and banks.
Expand distribution channels by identifying and activating non-branch avenues for gold loan acquisition.

Grow the Assets Under Management (AUM) for gold loan products while meeting revenue, cost, profitability, and portfolio quality targets.
Collaborate with the Product Head – Business Loan to offer comprehensive funding solutions and ensure DSTs are appropriately incentivized.
Ensure timely renewals of gold loan customers to retain and grow the customer base.
Maintain strong relationships with valuers and valuation agencies, ensuring timely rotation and compliance.
Train gold loan officers on fraud detection and identification of counterfeit ornaments.
Design a seamless and fulfilling customer journey to enhance satisfaction and position IDFC FIRST Bank as the preferred banking partner.
Ensure adherence to internal compliance and regulatory frameworks across all operations.


Education


Graduation: Full time graduate in any discipline from a recognized institution.


Experience


Minimum of years of experience in gold loan sales or related financial services within BFSI industry.

title: Lecturer / Assistant Professor
description:
AND Academy invites applications for the positions of Lecturer / Assistant Professor to join their academic team.




AND Academy is a design upskilling venture launched by the Indian Institute of Art and Design (IIAD), a leading design school in India established in collaboration with Kingston School of Art, London. We offer part-time Certificate, Diploma and Post-Graduate programs in Graphic Design, UX UI Design, and Interior Design, taught remotely online through live interactive sessions. With a curriculum developed by industry stalwarts and experienced academicians, our mission is to make learning design accessible, affordable, and industry relevant.




With more than a decade of experience in higher education, the team at AND Academy brings its expertise to online design education to create active, interactive, and collaborative learning experiences through live sessions and strategic tech integration. This role is for individuals who can adapt themselves to our learning-teaching methodology and bring along their professional expertise to help graduates and working professionals transform their careers through design training.

Minimum Qualifications

Bachelor's or Master's degree in Graphic Design / Communication Design from a reputable institution.
A minimum of 5 years of industry experience in the field
Some teaching experience will be considered an added advantage.




What we are looking for:

Strong understanding of fundamental concepts of design, as well as the concepts, processes, methods, techniques, and digital technology (including software skills) pertaining to the specialized field of Graphic Design / Publication Design / Brand Communication / UI Design
Domain knowledge should be backed with practical professional experience of using these in an industry & business context, as well as an updated knowledge of trends and technology.
A tech friendly attitude as you will be working on multiple digital platforms to collaborate and interact with colleagues as well as learners. If you are a tech-enthusiast, that will be an added bonus!




Role and Responsibility:

You will join our teaching team at AND Academy as a full-time faculty. Based on your area of expertise you will teach on any of our programmes–Graphic Design, UX UI Design.

Your main responsibility will be to implement our well-structured curriculum and course delivery plan through teaching, mentoring, and assessments.
This will be done through two roles:

>Module Instructor - i.e. teaching a module through interactive live sessions that include hands-on working

>Module Leader - i.e. being in charge of the instructional design for the module, and supervising the overall quality of delivery by other instructor

Besides conducting the live sessions (online classes) with students, this will also include developing study material, planning class interactions & activities, developing assignments & project briefs, providing constructive feedback (written / verbal), facilitating tech integration, and managing course delivery on our world-class Learning Management System.
You will also actively participate in course monitoring and enhancement activities to continually improve the quality of student learning and experience.
You should proactively seek opportunities to stay abreast of new techniques and processes in the area of your expertise through industry projects, research, conferences, seminars, workshops, certifications, etc.
You will be networking with the industry to facilitate student exposure, interactions, internships, and placements.
You will be required to collaborate with the admission team, corporate communications team, and career services team to contribute to student enrollment, brand building, and student placements through information and interactions.
Work collaboratively with colleagues demonstrating a high level of team spirit and contribute as an active member of a progressive community of practitioners.

Overall, we are looking for someone who is interested in still learning their craft and honing it above and beyond industry requirements and is excited to share their journey with peers and students.




Job Location – B26, Okhla Phase 1, New Delhi

Working Hours –

5 days per week: Monday-Friday
9 AM to 5 PM work from the office on days with no classes
On class Days: 9 AM to 2:30 PM work from the office and 7 PM - 10 PM from home
1 day per week work from home - after 2 months of starting work.

title: Associate - Asset Management Performance Attribution Reporting
description:
Job Title: Associate

Department: Asset Management – Investment Operations / Reporting

About Business line/Function: Asset Management Reporting is responsible for transforming validated investment, accounting, pricing, and reference data into accurate, timely, and compliant reports for clients, regulators, and internal stakeholders. The function supports multiple reporting types across asset classes, geographies, and regulatory regimes.

Position Purpose

The Validator role acts as the independent quality, control, and risk assurance layer within the Asset Management Reporting lifecycle. The role ensures that all reports produced are technically accurate, conceptually sound, methodologically consistent, and compliant with internal standards, client requirements, and regulatory expectations before final release.

The Validator is accountable for challenging outputs, validating assumptions, and preventing incorrect or misleading information from reaching clients or regulators.

Responsibilities

Direct Responsibilities


Perform independent validation of reports produced by Processors, focusing on accuracy, completeness, consistency, and appropriateness.
Review calculations, methodologies, disclosures, narratives, and trends across reporting periods.
Execute second-level controls and formally evidence validation and sign-off.
Ensure outputs align with documented procedures, regulatory guidance, and client expectations.


Contributing Responsibilities


Challenge unusual movements, overrides, or explanations and ensure they are supported by facts and documentation.
Identify systemic issues, recurring errors, or control weaknesses and escalate with root-cause analysis.
Support internal audits, external audits, and regulatory reviews with clear explanations and evidence.
Provide feedback and guidance to Processors to improve overall quality maturity.
Contribute to strengthening control frameworks and standardization.


Technical & Behavioral Competencies

Technical Competencies


Strong conceptual understanding of Asset Management Reporting, including performance, holdings, regulatory disclosures, and client reporting objectives.
Ability to interpret and validate complex data flows across investment, accounting, pricing, and reference data domains.
Solid knowledge of control frameworks, audit expectations, and risk management principles.
Capability to assess methodological appropriateness and not just numerical accuracy.


Behavioral Competencies


Demonstrates rigorous professional judgment, proactively questioning assumptions and constructively challenging perspectives to drive robust decision-making.
Strong attention to detail combined with a big-picture understanding of reporting impact.
Clear and structured communication, especially when explaining issues, risks, or decisions.
Strong ownership mindset with accountability for quality and risk prevention.
Ability to work under pressure during peak reporting cycles without compromising control standards.


Specific Qualifications

Post Graduate degree in Finance or a related discipline.

Strong understanding of performance measurement and attribution methodologies, including arithmetic and geometric attribution approaches.

Knowledge of key attribution models such as Brinson-Fachler / Brinson-Hood-Beebower, including allocation, selection, and interaction effects.

Familiarity with portfolio return calculations, benchmark comparison, and relative performance analysis across asset classes.

Ability to interpret and validate attribution results by linking performance drivers to investment decisions, market movements, and portfolio positioning.

Experience working with holdings, transactions, benchmark data, pricing, and FX data used in performance calculations.

Understanding of multi-asset and multi-currency portfolios, including impact of currency and derivative exposures on performance.

Ability to perform reconciliation and variance analysis between performance systems and reporting outputs.

Familiarity with performance attribution tools/platforms and reporting systems used in Asset Management.

Awareness of data dependencies, assumptions, and limitations within attribution models and reporting outputs.

Prior experience in Performance Attribution, Performance Measurement, or Investment Reporting roles is preferred.

Technical Skills

Skills Referential (Required knowledge, skills and abilities)


Asset Management Reporting concepts (performance, holdings, regulatory disclosures)
Data validation and reconciliation techniques
Advanced Excel; familiarity with reporting platforms and databases
Understanding of control frameworks and audit expectations
Understanding of asset management products and reporting methodologies

Behavioral Skills


Accountability and ownership
Analytical thinking
Clear written and verbal communication
Ability to work under peak delivery pressure


Risk awareness and judgement
Clear stakeholder communication
Quality and control orientation
Ability to challenge and influence positively

Education Level: Post Graduate degree in Finance

Candidates with equivalent professional experience in Asset Management Operations or Financial Reporting may be considered.

Location: Chennai , India

About BNP Paribas Group

BNP Paribas is the European Union’s leading bank and key player in international banking. It operates in 65 countries and has nearly 185,000 employees, including more than 145,000 in Europe. The Group has key positions in its three main fields of activity: Commercial, Personal Banking & Services for the Group’s commercial & personal banking and several specialized businesses including BNP Paribas Personal Finance and Arval; Investment & Protection Services for savings, investment, and protection solutions; and Corporate & Institutional Banking, focused on corporate and institutional clients. Based on its strong diversified and integrated model, the Group helps all its clients (individuals, community associations, entrepreneurs, SMEs, corporates and institutional clients) to realize their projects through solutions spanning financing, investment, savings and protection insurance. In Europe, BNP Paribas has four domestic markets: Belgium, France, Italy, and Luxembourg. The Group is rolling out its integrated commercial & personal banking model across several Mediterranean countries, Turkey, and Eastern Europe. As a key player in international banking, the Group has leading platforms and business lines in Europe, a strong presence in the Americas as well as a solid and fast-growing business in Asia-Pacific. BNP Paribas has implemented a Corporate Social Responsibility approach in all its activities, enabling it to contribute to the construction of a sustainable future, while ensuring the Group's performance and stability.

About BNP Paribas India Solutions

Established in 2005, BNP Paribas India Solutions is a wholly owned subsidiary of BNP Paribas SA, European Union’s leading bank with an international reach. title: Analyst I, Drugs
description:
Description

About Norstella

Norstella is a premier and critical global life sciences data and AI solutions provider dedicated to improving patient access to life-saving therapies. Norstella supports pharmaceutical and biotech companies across the full drug development lifecycle — from pipeline to patient. Our mission is simple: to help our clients bring therapies to market faster and more efficiently, ultimately impacting patient lives.

Norstella unites market-leading brands - Citeline, Evaluate, MMIT, Panalgo, Skipta and The Dedham Group and delivers must-have answers and insights, leveraging AI, for critical strategic, clinical, and commercial decision-making. We help our clients: 


Accelerate the drug development cycle 
Assess competition and bring the right drugs to market 
Make data driven commercial and financial decisions
Match and recruit patients for clinical trials
Identify and address barriers to therapies



 

Norstella serves most pharmaceutical and biotech companies around the world, along with regulators like the FDA, and payers. By providing critical proprietary data supporting AI-driven workflows, Norstella helps clients make decisions faster and with greater confidence. Norstella’s investments in AI are transforming how data is consumed and decisions are made, disrupting inefficient legacy workflows and helping the industry become more efficient, innovative, and responsive to patient needs.

Job Summary

The Analyst I is responsible for managing complex pharmaceutical data workflows, ensuring high-quality content delivery, and contributing to quality assurance, process enhancements, and team enablement. The role involves secondary research, stakeholder coordination, and internal reporting to support Norstella’s mission of delivering actionable pharma intelligence.

Responsibilities


Identify and extract key drug data from public sources (e.g., clinical trial registries, regulatory filings, company report)
Assess and track drug pipelines by stage, indication, and mechanism of action (MoA)
Interpret MoAs and connect them to disease pathways and clinical outcomes
Track regulatory designations and approvals (e.g., Orphan Drug, Breakthrough Therapy)
Validate and cross-check data entries across multiple sources for accuracy
Apply business rules, SOPs, and publishing workflows effectively using Fusion
Meet daily and weekly productivity targets while ensuring data quality
Manage multiple research tasks efficiently while maintaining quality and turnaround time



Qualifications


Graduate/Post-Graduate/Doctorate degree in Life Sciences, Pharmacy, Medical Sciences, or equivalent field
Minimum 3 years of work experience in business research and secondary data analysis, preferably within the life sciences or healthcare domain
Strong understanding of drug development processes, MoAs, indications, and regulatory pathways
Experience in using research tools and databases (e.g., ClinicalTrials.gov, EMA, FDA, SEC filings)
High proficiency in MS Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint); analytical and reporting skills.



Benefits


Health Insurance
Provident Fund
Life Insurance
Reimbursement of Certification Expenses
Gratuity
24x7 Health Desk



Our guiding principles for success at Norstella

01: Bold, Passionate, Mission-First

We have a lofty mission to Smooth Access to Life Saving Therapies and we will get there by being bold and passionate about the mission and our clients. Our clients and the mission in what we are trying to accomplish must be in the forefront of our minds in everything we do.

02: Integrity, Truth, Reality

We make promises that we can keep, and goals that push us to new heights. Our integrity offers us the opportunity to learn and improve by being honest about what works and what doesn’t. By being true to the data and producing realistic metrics, we are able to create plans and resources to achieve our goals.

03: Kindness, Empathy, Grace

We will empathize with everyone's situation, provide positive and constructive feedback with kindness, and accept opportunities for improvement with grace and gratitude. We use this principle across the organization to collaborate and build lines of open communication.

04: Resilience, Mettle, Perseverance

We will persevere – even in difficult and challenging situations. Our ability to recover from missteps and failures in a positive way will help us to be successful in our mission.

05: Humility, Gratitude, Learning

We will be true learners by showing humility and gratitude in our work. We recognize that the smartest person in the room is the one who is always listening, learning, and willing to shift their thinking.

Norstella is an equal opportunities employer and does not discriminate on the grounds of gender, sexual orientation, marital or civil partner status, pregnancy or maternity, gender reassignment, race, color, nationality, ethnic or national origin, religion or belief, disability or age. Our ethos is to respect and value people’s differences, to help everyone achieve more at work as well as in their personal lives so that they feel proud of the part they play in our success. We believe that all decisions about people at work should be based on the individual’s abilities, skills, performance and behavior and our business requirements. Norstella operates a zero tolerance policy to any form of discrimination, abuse or harassment.

Sometimes the best opportunities are hidden by self-doubt. We disqualify ourselves before we have the opportunity to be considered. Regardless of where you came from, how you identify, or the path that led you here- you are welcome. If you read this job description and feel passion and excitement, we’re just as excited about you.

All legitimate roles with Norstella will be posted on Norstella’s job board which is located at norstella.com/careers. If a role is not posted on this job board, a candidate should assume the role is not a legitimate role with Norstella. Norstella is not responsible for an application that may be submitted by or through a third-party and candidates should proceed with extreme caution if a third-party approaches them about an open role with Norstella. Norstella will never ask for anything of value or any type of payment during or as part of any recruitment, interview, or pre-hire onboarding process. Adani Power Limited (APL): Adani Power Limited (APL), a part of the diversified Adani Group, is the largest private thermal power producer in India. We have a power generation capacity of 15,250 MW comprising thermal power plants in Gujarat, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, and Jharkhand, and a 40 MW solar power project in Gujarat.

Job Purpose: This role is responsible to monitor and operate plant systems, including Boiler, Turbine, Generator, BOP, switchyard and transformer area ensuring their optimal performance and executing necessary start-up and shut-down procedures. This role also performs and monitors Flue Gas Desulfurization (FGD) operations, maintaining equipment, promoting energy conservation, and ensuring environmental compliance.

Responsibilities

Control Room Engineer/Lead Desk Engineer /Lead FGD/ Switchyard Engineer

System Monitoring And Operations

Monitor Boiler, Turbine, Generator, BOP, and Electrical Systems to ensure optimal performance.

Execute start-up and shut-down operations of the plant systems, as required.

Maintain close observation of plant parameters and respond promptly to any deviations.

Perform routine changeover of equipment and trial of emergency drives.

Emergency Response And Equipment Handling

Handle plant emergencies related to Boiler-Turbine-Generator (BTG) and coordinate responses.

Manage emergencies related to switchyards and grid problems to minimize impact on operations.

Ensure safe isolation and normalization of equipment in response to operational needs or emergencies.

Perform Flue Gas Desulfurization (FGD) operations and monitor FGD system parameters.

Maintain all plant parameter logbooks accurately and up-to-date.

Energy Conservation And Environmental Compliance

Promote energy conservation in all activities, focusing on Specific Oil Consumption (SOC), Auxiliary Power Consumption (APC), Demineralized (DM) Water usage, and Heat Rate.

Monitor critical chemistry parameters to ensure environmental compliance and operational efficiency.

Implement and oversee FGD operations to reduce emissions and comply with environmental regulations.

Business Sustainability

Ensure adherence to IMS, AWMS, DISHA, CHETNA guidelines within the department.

Maintain safety of personnel and equipment through proper training and adherence to safety protocols.

Adhere to Permit to Work (PTW) systems and Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).

Notify and report defects, problems in the plant to the shift in charge in a timely manner.

Digitization And Automation

Execute comprehensive digitization strategies to optimize operational efficiency.

Implement automation solutions to support overall organizational goals/strategy.

Implement process and system improvements, adopting newer technologies and innovative ideas.

Key Stakeholders - Internal

Maintenance Engineers

Key Stakeholders - External

NA

Qualifications

Educational Qualification:

BE/B.tech in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering or a related field from a recognized institution.

Work Experience (Range Of Years)

Minimum of 3+ years of experience in power plant operations with a focus on thermal power plants

Preferred Industry

Experience in the power generation industry, specifically with thermal power plants, is highly preferred.

description:
Job Description

Associates join as a cohort, receive intensive hands-on training across cutting-edge technology domains, and work on real Proof of Concept (POC) projects solving live Honeywell Aerospace business challenges.

At the end of the 12-month program, high-performing Associates graduate into permanent roles within the Honeywell Aerospace IT organization globally — making this one of the most direct pathways into a world-class technology career at a leading industrial aerospace company.

Responsibilities

Structured Technical Training (Months 1–4)


Participate in an intensive, hands-on training program designed and delivered by the Lead Enterprise Architect and senior Honeywell technologists
Build foundational and applied skills across priority technology domains:
Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning — Python, ML frameworks, GenAI, LLMs
ERP & S4 Hana — enterprise data, business process, and system architecture fundamentals
Data Engineering — data pipelines, cloud platforms, analytics, and visualization
DevOps & Cloud — CI/CD, containerization, cloud-native development
Complete assignments, labs, and assessments to demonstrate learning and earn your POC project assignment


POC Project Delivery (Months 4–10)


Work in small teams under the mentorship of the Lead Architect to design and build a POC solving a real Honeywell Aerospace business problem
Own a defined workstream within your POC — from problem definition through solution design, build, testing, and business presentation
Present your POC outcomes to senior IT and business leaders at the Lab's quarterly Demo Days
Collaborate with Honeywell business stakeholders (Commercial, Supply Chain, Finance, Engineering) to understand real-world problem context


Graduation & Career Transition (Months 10–12)


Work with the India Regions leadership team to identify your graduation role within the broader Honeywell Aerospace IT organization
Transition your POC learnings, documentation, and any ongoing workstreams to the receiving IT tower
Participate in the graduation showcase — presenting your Lab journey and outcomes to global IT leadership
Begin your permanent IT role within Honeywell Aerospace, fully equipped with hands-on experience and a proven track record


Qualifications

YOU MUST HAVE


Recent graduate (within 2 years) from a B.Tech, M.Tech, or MCA program from IIT, NIT, BITS Pilani, or equivalent Tier 1/Tier 2 engineering institution
Strong academic performance with demonstrated technical aptitude in Computer Science, Information Technology, Electronics, or related disciplines
Hands-on experience with programming (Python, Java, or equivalent) through coursework, projects, or internships
Genuine curiosity for technology and a desire to solve real-world problems — not just pass exams
Collaborative mindset with strong communication skills in English


WE VALUE


Prior internship experience in a technology company or startup
Exposure to AI/ML, cloud platforms (AWS/Azure/GCP), or SAP through coursework or self-learning
Participation in hackathons, technical competitions, or open-source projects
Demonstrated leadership in academic clubs, technical societies, or team projects


What You Will Gain


12 months of structured, immersive technical training delivered by senior Honeywell architects and industry experts
Hands-on experience building real solutions for a Fortune 100 Aerospace technology organization
Mentorship from senior technology leaders across Honeywell globally
A direct pathway into a permanent role within Honeywell Aerospace IT upon successful graduation
The Honeywell brand, network, and experience as a foundation for a world-class technology career


#AERO26

About Us

Honeywell helps organizations solve the world's most complex challenges in automation, the future of aviation and energy transition. As a trusted partner, we provide actionable solutions and innovation through our Aerospace Technologies, Building Automation, Energy and Sustainability Solutions, and Industrial Automation business segments – powered by our Honeywell Forge software – that help make the world smarter, safer and more sustainable.

title: Analyst/Associate Consultant - Corporate Treasury
description:
Financial Services Risk Management, Corporate Treasury Advisory – Analyst or ACon




About the Company




KPMG is a global network of professional firms providing Audit, Tax and Advisory services. We operate in 156 countries and have 152,000 people working in member firms around the world. KPMG in India, a professional services firm, is the Indian member firm of KPMG International and was established in September 1993. Our professionals leverage the global network of firms, providing detailed knowledge of local laws, regulations, markets and competition. KPMG in India provide services to over 4,500 international and national clients, in India. KPMG has offices across India in Delhi, Chandigarh, Ahmedabad, Mumbai, Pune, Chennai, Bangalore, Kochi, Hyderabad and Kolkata. The Indian firm has access to more than 7,000 Indian and expatriate professionals, many of whom are internationally trained. We strive to provide rapid, performance-based, industry-focused and technology-enabled services, which reflect a shared knowledge of global and local industries and our experience of the Indian business environment. KPMG Advisory professionals provide advice and assistance to enable companies, intermediaries and public sector bodies to mitigate risk, improve performance, and create value. KPMG firms provide a wide range of Risk Consulting, Management Consulting and Transactions & Restructuring services that can help clients respond to immediate needs as well as put in place the strategies for the longer term. With increasing regulatory requirements, the need for greater transparency in operations, and disclosure norms, stakeholders require assurance beyond the traditional critique of numbers. Hence assurance is being increasingly required on industry issues, business risks and key business processes. The Governance, Risk & Compliance Services practice assists companies and public sector bodies to mitigate risk, improve performance and create value. We assist our clients to effectively manage business and process risks by providing a full spectrum of corporate governance, risk management, and Compliance Services. These services are tailored to meet client’s individual needs, and provide effective support to management in meeting the challenges and opportunities presented by today's complex business environment. Our professionals provide the experience to help companies stay on track and deal with risks that could unhinge their business survival. Our services enable clients to effectively co-ordinate their key growth, quality and operational challenges and working in partnership with us, clients have the benefits of KPMG's experienced, objective, and industry-grounded viewpoints.




Key skills and qualifications

Bachelor degree like Bachelor in Business Administration/ Bachelor in Financial Accounting/ Bachelor in Banking and Insurance from a Tier 1 college/ institute along with FRM (both level cleared) in first attempt and/ or Chartered Financial Analyst from GARP
Strong academic record at 10, 10+2 and graduate examinations
Good understanding of corporate treasury operations and financial products
Understanding of Spot and Derivative market operations for interest rate, foreign exchange products including risk management (high level hedging strategies) and valuation aspects of the same
Well-versed in MS Excel, MS PowerPoint and Power BI. Should be able to build MS Excel based models for risk quantifications using various methods.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills
Leadership experience at school/college level preferable
High levels of commitment to work




Role and responsibilities

The candidate will be expected to assist assignments involving assisting Indian and international corporate organizations in their treasury operations
The candidate will be expected to assist Assistant Managers to Senior Managers in Corporate Treasury assignments including advisory / Internal Audit/ review of treasury operations areas such as foreign exchange risk management, interest rate risk management, commodities risk management, cash and liquidity management, borrowings management, investments management.
The candidate should be well versed with concepts like Value at Risk and its computational methodologies, Expected shortfall method, Sensitivity analysis, etc. Candidate should understand key concepts of financial risk management.
The assignments would include providing advisory / conducting reviews for the areas mentioned above covering strategy, policy, governance, day to day operations, process improvement opportunities, compliance to internal directives and regulatory requirements etc.
The candidate will be expected to take-up business development initiatives and assisting in creating proposals, responses to RFPs, thought leaderships, etc.
The candidate will be expected to have very strong communication skills and confidence to interact independently with junior to mid-level client personnel.
The position is based out of Mumbai and the candidate must be open to travel




Compensation is competitive with industry standards. Details of the compensation breakup will be shared with short-listed candidates only.

The opportunity is now! If you are interested in being part of a dynamic team, serving clients and reaching your full potential – KPMG Advisory Services is for you!

description:

This role also involves advanced troubleshooting, configuration management, and problem resolution for complex IT systems, often involving escalations from L2 support and working with various stakeholders. Analyzing scan results to identify security weaknesses and misconfigurations.

Risk Prioritization and Remediation:

Documentation and Reporting:

Collaboration and Communication:

Communicating the risks associated with vulnerabilities to stakeholders and providing guidance on mitigation strategies.
Mentoring and guiding junior team members on vulnerability management principles and practices.


Skills And Experience


Strong understanding of information security principles and practices.
Proficiency in vulnerability scanning tools and techniques.
Experience with Risk Assessment methodologies and vulnerability scoring systems.

Excellent communication, collaboration, and problem-solving skills.

title: Data Scientist
description:
Who is this for




If building sophisticated statistical models and conducting rigorous causal analysis to drive business impact excites you, this is your opportunity. Fornax is seeking a Data Scientist who combines advanced analytical techniques with business acumen to solve complex challenges in the Retail domain.

We are looking for a technically proficient data scientist who excels at experimental design, and predictive modelling while translating complex methodologies into actionable business insights.




Role & responsibilities




Data Science & Modeling (50%)




Build and deploy machine learning models for pricing optimization, demand forecasting, and promotional response prediction
Develop predictive models using regression, decision trees, gradient boosting (XGBoost, LightGBM), and neural networks
Create time series forecasting models (ARIMA, Prophet, LSTM) for demand and revenue prediction
Build optimization algorithms for pricing, assortment, and trade spend allocation
Conduct causal inference analysis to measure true promotional incrementality and long-term effects
Develop customer segmentation using clustering algorithms and propensity models for churn, LTV, and next-best-action
Perform market basket analysis and product affinity modeling
Create recommendation engines for product and promotion personalization
Implement A/B testing frameworks and experimental design for pricing and promotional strategies
Conduct feature engineering, model validation, and hyperparameter tuning
Deploy models into production and monitor performance over time




Revenue Growth Management Analytics (30%)




Develop price elasticity models and optimize price-pack architecture across channels and segments
Build promotional effectiveness models analyzing lift, ROI, and baseline incrementality
Create trade spend optimization models to maximize return on investment
Analyze portfolio performance and build SKU rationalization frameworks
Support fact-based retailer negotiations with predictive insights and scenario simulations
Conduct gross-to-net revenue analysis and identify revenue leakage patterns
Build what-if scenario planning tools for pricing, promotion, and assortment strategies




Visualization & Communication (15%)




Design Power BI dashboards to communicate model insights and predictions
Translate complex model outputs into actionable business recommendations
Present findings to senior leadership with compelling data storytelling
Write SQL queries to extract and prepare data for modeling
Create automated reporting for model performance and business KPIs




Collaboration & Mentorship (5%)




Partner with commercial, finance, and marketing teams to define modeling requirements
Document model methodologies, assumptions, and technical specifications
Mentor junior analysts and data scientists on best practices
Drive continuous improvement in modeling approaches and analytical frameworks







Key Qualifications

Education: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science, Engineering, or related quantitative field. Advanced degree preferred.




Experience: 3-6 years of experience in analytics or data science, with at least 2 years focused on Revenue Growth Management, pricing analytics, trade promotion optimization, or commercial analytics in Retail/D2C/FMCG/CPG domains

Technical Skills:




Expert-level Power BI: DAX, Power Query, data modeling, dashboard design, custom visualizations
Advanced SQL: Complex queries, window functions, CTEs, query optimization, performance tuning
Python/R Programming: Data manipulation (pandas, numpy), visualization (matplotlib, seaborn, plotly), statistical analysis
Machine Learning: Scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM, TensorFlow/PyTorch basics
Statistical Analysis: Regression (linear, logistic, polynomial), hypothesis testing, A/B testing, experimental design

As a Senior Consultant in a Digital Strategy role at PwC India, your aim is threefold:

to support clients throughout the discover, design, and target state phases of (digital) strategy engagements. Based on your previous work experience (strategist in digital consulting or tech consulting company, business analyst, product owner for digital solutions) you can collate insights on business objectives, customer needs, and technical solutions and translate these into impactful digital concepts. You work with a team of consultants on the topic of digital strategy: focused on delivery of projects, internal knowledge sharing and overall practice support. Finally, you are also willing to engage on the topic from a commercial perspective. In conjoint effort with the rest of the senior management team, you support in the development of new business proposals. At the level of senior consultant, this is mostly done by managing risk, developing high quality deliverables, and building relationships with junior stakeholders at your clients.

You do this as part of our broader multidisciplinary team (Strategist, Technology Experts, Enterprise Architects, Project Management Professionals, Domain Experts, Sector Experts)

You are the day-to-day point of contact for clients and Managers on projects. You work with client teams to deliver high quality outputs;

A relevant Master’s degree, preferably with an organisational/ business focus and/or technical focus
Strong business sense, analytical skills, and numerical understanding

title: Business Analyst-AI & Analytics
description:
Why Mizuho

At Mizuho, we provide the stability of an international industry leader with the career trajectory of a growing business. Our steady, strategic growth gives our people at all levels rewarding degrees of responsibility and richer work experience than a boutique firm or an established giant could offer alone.




It’s the local expertise of our employees that makes our global network so powerful. By collaborating with colleagues and clients who have the same ambition and drive, you can amplify your sphere of influence and base of knowledge as part of one of the largest—and growing—banks in the world.




Role Summary:




The AI & Analytics team is seeking an experienced Business Analyst to play a critical role in shaping, governing, and delivering enterprise‑scale AI, analytics, and intelligent automation initiatives across the bank. This role requires a strong blend of business analysis expertise, financial services domain knowledge, and the ability to operate independently with senior stakeholders.

The Business Analyst will lead complex requirements efforts, influence solution design, support AI governance and risk processes, and ensure analytics and AI solutions deliver measurable business value. The role is expected to demonstrate strong judgment, ownership, and leadership, while serving as a trusted partner to front‑office, risk, compliance, operations, and technology teams.




Key Responsibilities:




Business Analysis & Solution Definition

Lead end‑to‑end business analysis for complex AI, analytics, and intelligent automation initiatives, from problem definition through delivery and adoption.
Own the translation of business strategy and operating needs into clear use cases, functional requirements, user stories, and success metrics.
Partner closely with AI engineers, data engineers, and analytics leads to shape solution approaches and ensure alignment with business intent.
Challenge assumptions and proactively recommend alternative approaches where appropriate.

Senior Stakeholder Engagement

Act as a primary point of contact for senior business and technology stakeholders across multiple lines of business.
Facilitate working sessions, design reviews, and decision forums with executive‑level participants.
Provide clear, concise communication on scope, risks, trade‑offs, and delivery status, tailored to senior audiences.

Governance, Risk & Regulatory Alignment

Play a key role in AI and analytics governance, including model intake, approvals, documentation, and ongoing monitoring.
Ensure solutions comply with internal policies, model risk management standards, data governance requirements, and applicable regulatory expectations.
Contribute to materials for governance committees, audits, regulatory exams, and internal reviews.
Act as a control‑minded partner, identifying and escalating risks early and pragmatically.

Delivery Leadership & Execution

Provide analytical leadership across one or more workstreams within large or strategically important initiatives.
Support planning, dependency management, issue resolution, and delivery tracking in partnership with program and delivery leads.
Oversee user acceptance testing (UAT), validation of business outcomes, and readiness for production deployment.
Mentor junior analysts and provide informal guidance on analysis standards and best practices.

Business Value & Insight Generation

Analyze business processes, operational metrics, and performance data to identify opportunities for AI‑driven improvement.
Support development of business cases, value realization frameworks, and benefits tracking for AI and analytics initiatives.
Prepare executive‑ready presentations, summaries, and written materials for senior management and governance forums.




Required Experience

6–10 years of experience as a Business Analyst or similar role within financial services, investment banking, or capital markets.
Proven experience leading analysis for data, analytics, or AI‑enabled initiatives of meaningful scale and complexity.
Demonstrated experience operating in highly regulated environments with strong governance and control requirements.




Required Skills:




Advanced requirements elicitation, documentation, and stakeholder management skills.
Strong understanding of data, analytics, and AI concepts, including data flows, model lifecycles, and analytics delivery practices.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (PowerPoint, Excel); experience with Agile delivery tools (e.g., Jira, Confluence) preferred.
Excellent executive‑level written and verbal communication skills.
Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and initiatives concurrently.




Key Competencies:




Strategic & Analytical Thinking: Ability to connect analytics initiatives to broader business strategy and outcomes.
Leadership & Ownership: Takes accountability for results and operates with minimal oversight.
Stakeholder Influence: Trusted advisor capable of influencing senior business and technology leaders.
Risk Awareness & Judgment: Demonstrates sound judgment in balancing innovation, control, and regulatory considerations.




Qualifications:




Bachelor’s degree in business, Finance, Information Systems, Data Analytics, or a related field.
Advanced degree (MBA, MS) and/or relevant certifications (CBAP, PMI‑PBA, Agile BA) preferred.




Organization Overview:




Mizuho Global Services (MGS), Pune is an integral part of Mizuho Financial Group, one of the world’s leading financial institutions with a strong global presence across the Americas, EMEA, and Asia. Based in India, MGS Pune supports Mizuho’s international businesses by delivering high-quality, scalable, and resilient services across multiple functions.




MGS Pune plays a critical role in driving operational excellence, standardization, and innovation for Mizuho Americas. By combining deep domain expertise with strong process, technology, and analytical capabilities, it partners closely with regional and global teams to support corporate and investment banking, capital markets, and corporate services functions, while adhering to the highest standards of risk management, regulatory compliance, and control.




MGS Pune offers competitive compensation and benefits package aligned with industry standards and local market practices.




MGS Pune is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace.




Employment is subject to applicable background verification checks in accordance with Indian laws and company policies.

description:

About The Role

To ensure smooth, efficient, and high-quality production of automotive Connector Car and Car Audio products through effective planning, monitoring and optimization of the assembly lines, Act for implementation of production processes and procedures, leading productivity improvements, including new product introduction and manufacturing cell design to Reduce waste, Improve Safety, Quality, Delivery Performance. Diploma/B.E./ B. Tech in Mechanical, Electronics, Electrical or related.

Strong problem-solving, analytical thinking, team coordination and communication.

Be willing to work in office based on up dependencies

Extensive training opportunities through our own HARMAN University
Competitive wellness benefits

An inclusive and diverse work environment that fosters and encourages professional and personal development


You Belong Here

HARMAN is committed to making every employee feel welcomed, valued, and empowered. No matter what role you play, we encourage you to share your ideas, voice your distinct perspective, and bring your whole self with you – all within a support-minded culture that celebrates what makes each of us unique. We also recognize that learning is a lifelong pursuit and want you to flourish. We proudly offer added opportunities for training, development, and continuing education, further empowering you to live the career you want.

About HARMAN: Where Innovation Unleashes Next-Level Technology

Ever since the 1920s, we’ve been amplifying the sense of sound. Today, that legacy endures, with integrated technology platforms that make the world smarter, safer, and more connected.

Across automotive, lifestyle, and digital transformation solutions, we create innovative technologies that turn ordinary moments into extraordinary experiences. Our renowned automotive and lifestyle solutions can be found everywhere, from the music we play in our cars and homes to venues that feature today’s most sought-after performers, while our digital transformation solutions serve humanity by addressing the world’s ever-evolving needs and demands. Marketing our award-winning portfolio under 16 iconic brands, such as JBL, Mark Levinson, and Revel, we set ourselves apart by exceeding the highest engineering and design standards for our customers, our partners and each other.

If you’re ready to innovate and do work that makes a lasting impact, join our talent community today!

New Delhi

Job Description

Job title: Analyst/Senior Analyst (TMT)

Company Description

We’re L.E.K. Consulting, a global strategy consultancy working with business leaders to seize competitive advantage and amplify growth. Our insights are catalysts that reshape the trajectory of our clients’ businesses, uncovering opportunities and empowering them to master their moments of truth. Looking for more? Visit

Position Overview

The L.E.K. Capability Network (LCN) is an integrated part of L.E.K. Consulting, collaborating closely with consulting teams in L.E.K.’s worldwide network of offices to support them on their toughest and most impactful assignments.

The Analyst / Senior Analyst at LCN plays a critical role in supporting case teams by leading analytical modules, managing research initiatives, and providing insightful recommendations.

A typical project might involve an in-depth primary research campaign to identify and triage potential experts to speak to in a particular field, before holding discussions with them to discover their insights on a topic. It could involve rigorous secondary research to prove or disprove a client hypothesis, leveraging the full arsenal of L.E.K. toolkits and databases. Or it could mean undertaking a critical analysis to illuminate a pattern from the noise around it, to substantiate a strategic decision. They are expected to take greater ownership of their work, proactively structure analysis, and effectively communicate findings. This role requires strong problem-solving skills, the ability to manage multiple tasks across time zones, and a collaborative mindset to mentor junior analysts and contribute to L.E.K.'s culture. More information can be found at

We are currently hiring for Analyst/Senior Analysts into the LCN, a role which will report into the LCN Team lead/Team manager

Primary Research

Responsibilities will include, but are not limited to:


Manage interview campaign including creating IV guide, expert identification, managing expert networks and other vendors, and manage real-time update
Independently work with consulting staff on projects by sourcing content experts and key opinion leaders (e.g., with LinkedIn, expert networks, cold calling, etc.)
Write solicitations for experts to conduct interviews with case teams, and custom solicitations for key opinion leaders (e.g., referring to papers he / she has published)
Conduct local / international interview campaigns and summarize key takeaways from the interview to help case teams derive strategic conclusions
Extract and curate key insights from interviews to validate or challenge case hypotheses, driving informed decision-making


Secondary research


Own end-to-end secondary research modules, identifying relevant data sources and extracting key insights
Collaborate with teams to identify secondary research resources that address client areas of interest (e.g., journal articles, marketing / industry reports, government publications)
Confirm/disprove case team hypotheses, deriving insights and patterns from unstructured problem statements using publicly available reports, articles, thought pieces, company filings and GenAI
Independently contribute to case teams by applying structured problem-solving techniques to test and validate case hypotheses


Analysis: Modelling / surveys


Manage and deliver end-to-end survey analysis including coding, testing, analysis and curating insights
Co-develop comprehensive models such as market models, revenue models, and cost models to support various due-diligence cases
Utilize Excel, Alteryx, Tableau, and other analytical tools to structure data and create compelling visualizations
Review client data to understand gaps in datasets


Slide preparation and quality control


Design and recommend analytical frameworks to effectively present insights in a clear, accurate, and impactful manner
Develop clear and compelling commentary that extracts actionable insights
Create sophisticated visuals in PowerPoint and Tableau for consulting teams to use in client presentations
Collaborate with L.E.K. Graphic Design team to recommend visually interesting layouts for showing complex data
Review slide formatting for quality control, making layouts consistent and in line with L.E.K. brand standards (e.g., updating L.E.K. IP to current visual standards)


Coaching and mentorship


Provide support and oversight to junior analysts on projects, ensuring they meet project objectives
Coach junior analysts on technical skillsets such as secondary research using databases, effective slide writing and data analysis
Share industry insights and best practices with team members to enhance their skills and knowledge.


Case closure support


Prepare redacted versions of materials for delivery to KM
Prepare PowerPoint release documents for case team to deliver to client
Facilitate access agreements process for teams interacting with investors (e.g., as part of a due diligence)


Qualifications


The ideal candidate will have 3-5 years of relevant experience with a top-tier consulting firm
An undergraduate degree is required
Fair understanding of business concepts and their practical applications
Ability to work on multiple tasks and activities at once and flexibility to re-prioritize
Expert PowerPoint and Excel skills
Strong communication skills, both written and verbal
High attention to detail


Benefits And Perks


L.E.K. offers you the opportunity to work in a hybrid setup
We are among the best paying firms globally
Generous time away
We have Parental leaves (Maternity and Paternity) and flexible work options for new mothers
We provide childcare facilities in Mumbai (Day care / Creche services).
Wellness benefits – Health Insurance, Wellness Apps, Gym reimbursement, etc.

title: Analyst/Associate Consultant - Corporate Treasury
description:
Financial Services Risk Management, Corporate Treasury Advisory – Analyst or ACon




About the Company




KPMG is a global network of professional firms providing Audit, Tax and Advisory services. We operate in 156 countries and have 152,000 people working in member firms around the world. KPMG in India, a professional services firm, is the Indian member firm of KPMG International and was established in September 1993. Our professionals leverage the global network of firms, providing detailed knowledge of local laws, regulations, markets and competition. KPMG in India provide services to over 4,500 international and national clients, in India. KPMG has offices across India in Delhi, Chandigarh, Ahmedabad, Mumbai, Pune, Chennai, Bangalore, Kochi, Hyderabad and Kolkata. The Indian firm has access to more than 7,000 Indian and expatriate professionals, many of whom are internationally trained. We strive to provide rapid, performance-based, industry-focused and technology-enabled services, which reflect a shared knowledge of global and local industries and our experience of the Indian business environment. KPMG Advisory professionals provide advice and assistance to enable companies, intermediaries and public sector bodies to mitigate risk, improve performance, and create value. KPMG firms provide a wide range of Risk Consulting, Management Consulting and Transactions & Restructuring services that can help clients respond to immediate needs as well as put in place the strategies for the longer term. With increasing regulatory requirements, the need for greater transparency in operations, and disclosure norms, stakeholders require assurance beyond the traditional critique of numbers. Hence assurance is being increasingly required on industry issues, business risks and key business processes. The Governance, Risk & Compliance Services practice assists companies and public sector bodies to mitigate risk, improve performance and create value. We assist our clients to effectively manage business and process risks by providing a full spectrum of corporate governance, risk management, and Compliance Services. These services are tailored to meet client’s individual needs, and provide effective support to management in meeting the challenges and opportunities presented by today's complex business environment. Our professionals provide the experience to help companies stay on track and deal with risks that could unhinge their business survival. Our services enable clients to effectively co-ordinate their key growth, quality and operational challenges and working in partnership with us, clients have the benefits of KPMG's experienced, objective, and industry-grounded viewpoints.




Key skills and qualifications

Bachelor degree like Bachelor in Business Administration/ Bachelor in Financial Accounting/ Bachelor in Banking and Insurance from a Tier 1 college/ institute along with FRM (both level cleared) in first attempt and/ or Chartered Financial Analyst from GARP
Strong academic record at 10, 10+2 and graduate examinations
Good understanding of corporate treasury operations and financial products
Understanding of Spot and Derivative market operations for interest rate, foreign exchange products including risk management (high level hedging strategies) and valuation aspects of the same
Well-versed in MS Excel, MS PowerPoint and Power BI. Should be able to build MS Excel based models for risk quantifications using various methods.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills
Leadership experience at school/college level preferable
High levels of commitment to work




Role and responsibilities

The candidate will be expected to assist assignments involving assisting Indian and international corporate organizations in their treasury operations
The candidate will be expected to assist Assistant Managers to Senior Managers in Corporate Treasury assignments including advisory / Internal Audit/ review of treasury operations areas such as foreign exchange risk management, interest rate risk management, commodities risk management, cash and liquidity management, borrowings management, investments management.
The candidate should be well versed with concepts like Value at Risk and its computational methodologies, Expected shortfall method, Sensitivity analysis, etc. Candidate should understand key concepts of financial risk management.
The assignments would include providing advisory / conducting reviews for the areas mentioned above covering strategy, policy, governance, day to day operations, process improvement opportunities, compliance to internal directives and regulatory requirements etc.
The candidate will be expected to take-up business development initiatives and assisting in creating proposals, responses to RFPs, thought leaderships, etc.
The candidate will be expected to have very strong communication skills and confidence to interact independently with junior to mid-level client personnel.
The position is based out of Mumbai and the candidate must be open to travel

Reporting to the Global Manager, Data Access Control, the Data Access Control Analyst is the driver of all technical aspects of the Data Governance team. title: Investment Banking Analyst
description:
Investment Banking Analyst (Graduates and Experienced Hires) - Bengaluru

Please apply to Mumbai on our separate vacancy if that is closer to your location







Our Client

Our subsidiary CF Invest, is a dedicated recruitment firm focused on positions in the investment sector.




We are recruiting on behalf of our client, one of the bulge bracket bank and pre-eminent investment banks.




This tier one firm works with us to select the highest calibre talent in the global markets at entry and experienced entry level across their global offices on an ongoing basis through the first half of each year, with the view of onboarding immediately or upon graduation.




Our client is looking for two professionals to join their Investment Banking team in Bengaluru as an Analyst and use their skills and ideas to make an impact on the success of the bank. You will either be placed in their FIG, TMT or Energy Group or possibly another division as they also seek cross industry including but not limited to Industrials, Healthcare and Consumer.




The role requires talent to advise clients on various financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, equity/equity-linked and debt financings, joint ventures, restructurings, and financial investments.







Your Focus

As an Investment Banking Analyst, your focus is on supporting clients through complex capital markets and advisory transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, and capital raising. You will work closely with senior bankers to analyse opportunities, structure transactions, and help clients make informed strategic decisions.




The role is execution-driven and detail-intensive. You will build and maintain financial analyses, support transaction execution, and contribute to client materials used in live deals. You will also help assess risk, ensure adherence to regulatory and compliance requirements, and maintain the highest standards of accuracy, judgement, and professionalism.




This role suits someone who is analytically rigorous, commercially aware, and motivated by working at pace on high-stakes transactions. You will be expected to take ownership of your work, communicate clearly with colleagues and clients, and contribute meaningfully to deal delivery in a demanding, regulated environment.







Responsibilities

Support the execution of investment banking transactions, including M&A, capital raising, and strategic advisory mandates
Build and maintain detailed financial models and analyses to evaluate transactions and strategic options
Prepare client-facing materials, including pitch decks, information memoranda, and transaction documentation
Conduct company, industry, and market research to support deal origination and execution
Assist senior bankers in coordinating transaction workstreams and managing timelines
Support due diligence processes, including data analysis and coordination with internal and external stakeholders
Contribute to client development activities through research, analysis, and preparation of marketing materials
Assess transaction-related risks and support adherence to regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements
Maintain high standards of accuracy, organisation, and confidentiality across all work
Work effectively with colleagues across banking, markets, legal, risk, and compliance teams




This role is about supporting the execution of complex investment banking transactions and helping clients make informed strategic and capital decisions.




You can absorb detailed financial and commercial information quickly, analyse companies, transactions, and market conditions with rigour, and form clear, well-reasoned conclusions. You approach problems with structure and discipline, and you are comfortable working under pressure as deals progress and priorities evolve.







Minimum Qualifications




Graduate – Analyst

Bachelor’s degree ideally in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline. We accept also individuals from any degree, especially those numerate based
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear client-ready materials
High attention to detail and strong organisational skills
Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities
Sound judgement and a strong understanding of risk, compliance, and regulatory expectations
We consider any degree or subject and although your grade indicates your ability to absorb data and apply it, your degree grade does not influence your chance of applying to us and being interviewed.







Process

This application shall be open until we reach a cap of applicants, so please apply early. We are hiring for our client across their key global markets.




Be sure to Follow Us on LinkedIn for updates on this vacancy and others: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cfcorp




Your first step is to apply with your LinkedIn profile and CV. We do not need a covering letter. Due to the volume of applications we receive, we are unfortunately unable to provide individual feedback.







Reward Structure

Analyst annual base salary of 125,000 and up to 150,000 United States Dollars depending on experience. Your salary will be paid in Indian Rupees and bonus in USD
Bonus program of on-target bonus of 50% of base salary and up to 125% for high-achievers
Relocation is provided by the company

title: Program Associate (Talent and Partnerships)
description:
Avistrat works with aerospace engineers and organisations to build design capability and deploy engineering capacity. We engage with industry across two dimensions of scale:




Vertical scale — by deepening technical capability, and execution layers within specific aerospace domains.
Horizontal scale — by expanding across teams, programmes, and partner organisations where credible engineering capacity is required.




On the capability side, we support organisations by augmenting their design engineering functions and strengthening technical depth through structured, project-oriented engagement.

On the capacity side, we work closely with industry partners to identify, prepare, and deploy engineering talent aligned to real-world aerospace requirements.




Our work sits at the intersection of:




Engineering design
Industry readiness
Talent deployment




We could be called an EdTech, SkillsTech or an engineering services company but are building Avistrat as a scalable, integrated aerospace company — one that grows in depth, breadth, and responsibility while maintaining high standards of quality, credibility, and long-term partnership.

This is a high-rigour, outcome-driven environment where judgment, ownership, and execution matter more than volume or optics.




ROLE OVERVIEW




This is an entry-level, execution-focused role providing hands-on exposure to how talent pipelines and client delivery are executed in the aerospace domain.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES




Candidate Intake & Decision Support:




- Own the first line of evaluation for candidates engaging with Avistrat

- Conduct structured conversations to assess readiness, seriousness, and fit

- Build judgment on who should move forward and who should not

- Prepare concise recommendations that directly inform leadership decisions




Talent Pipeline Ownership:




- Build and manage a live talent pipeline aligned to industry needs

- Shortlist candidates for internships, entry-level roles, and capacity-building mandates

- Coordinate execution from shortlisting to interview outcomes

- Track progress, identify bottlenecks, and close gaps proactively




Client & Partner Interface (Execution-Led):




- Support delivery of client capacity-building mandates

- Coordinate candidate–client interactions and timelines

- Gain exposure to real industry-facing execution with increasing responsibility




Process Building & Continuous Improvement:




- Convert live execution into repeatable processes and templates

- Analyse and improve tracking of candidates, conversations, and outcomes

- Contribute to making Avistrat’s talent pipeline scalable and predictable




WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT




Not a sales role
Not a call-centre counselling role
Not an HR Admin role




WHAT WE LOOK FOR




A background and interest in the People or Human Resources function (academic or professional) is a must
Experience or demonstrated comfort with recruitment is a must
Some experience with aerospace would be ideal
Clear verbal and written communication
Comfort with ownership and follow-through
Ability to learn by doing and exploring fresh ideas
Ability to make and defend structured judgements
Attention to detail and professional conduct
Growth and improvement mindset
Drive to achieve goals




WHAT YOU WILL LEARN




How to thrive in a multi-stakeholder work environment
How to manage trade-offs for strategic decision-making
How quality of talent directly affects delivery outcomes
What enables successful client engagement
What the various elements of business operations are
How to grow and take ownership to drive work streams




GROWTH PATH




Freshers and early hires will join as interns for the first 3 months. Subsequently, the growth path could be expected as below:




0–6 months: Junior Talent and Partnerships Programme Associate (execution and judgment building)
6–12 months: Expanded ownership of intake, pipeline, and client delivery
12–18 months: Eligibility for progression to lead role, based on performance and business needs




There are various possible avenues along which career progression can be made based on candidate aspirations and business needs:




Ownership of the talent pipeline of Avistrat and organisational HR management
Programme management to manage a business function within Avistrat’s operating structure
Founder’s Office team to gain first-hand entrepreneurship insights




For further details about us, please visit our website: www.avistrat.com

title: Investment Banking Summer Analyst
description:
This role is for the Bengaluru office. Those across India can apply.







Our Client

Our subsidiary Global Markets Recruitment (“GMR”) is a dedicated recruitment firm focused on emerging and developed markets at entry to middle-management level.




We have been asked to asked by our client to run a campaign to identify entry level interns for this summer 2026. Our client is a bulge bracket bank and one of the pre-eminent investment banks.




This tier one firm works with us to select the highest calibre talent in the global markets at entry and experienced entry level across their global offices. We will run campaigns for them on an ongoing basis through the first half of this year.




Our client is looking for people in their global offices and we will post these roles for each location. Please apply to the location closest to you.




You will either be placed in their FIG or TMT Group or possibly another division as they also seek cross industry people also in energy, Industrials, Healthcare and Consumer.




The role requires talent to advise clients on various financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, equity/equity-linked and debt financings, joint ventures, restructurings, and financial investments.







Your Focus

As an Investment Banking Analyst - Intern, your focus is on supporting clients through complex capital markets and advisory transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, and capital raising. You will work closely with senior bankers to analyse opportunities, structure transactions, and help clients make informed strategic decisions.




The role is execution-driven and detail-intensive. You will build and maintain financial analyses, support transaction execution, and contribute to client materials used in live deals. You will also help assess risk, ensure adherence to regulatory and compliance requirements, and maintain the highest standards of accuracy, judgement, and professionalism.




This role suits someone who is analytically rigorous, commercially aware, and motivated by working at pace on high-stakes transactions. You will be expected to take ownership of your work, communicate clearly with colleagues and clients, and contribute meaningfully to deal delivery in a demanding, regulated environment.







Responsibilities

Support the execution of investment banking transactions, including M&A, capital raising, and strategic advisory mandates
Build and maintain detailed financial models and analyses to evaluate transactions and strategic options
Prepare client-facing materials, including pitch decks, information memoranda, and transaction documentation
Conduct company, industry, and market research to support deal origination and execution
Assist senior bankers in coordinating transaction workstreams and managing timelines
Support due diligence processes, including data analysis and coordination with internal and external stakeholders
Contribute to client development activities through research, analysis, and preparation of marketing materials
Assess transaction-related risks and support adherence to regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements
Maintain high standards of accuracy, organisation, and confidentiality across all work
Work effectively with colleagues across banking, markets, legal, risk, and compliance teams




This role is about supporting the execution of complex investment banking transactions and helping clients make informed strategic and capital decisions.




You can absorb detailed financial and commercial information quickly, analyse companies, transactions, and market conditions with rigour, and form clear, well-reasoned conclusions. You approach problems with structure and discipline, and you are comfortable working under pressure as deals progress and priorities evolve.







Minimum Qualifications

Graduate – Analyst

At university studying any degree ideally in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline. We accept also individuals from any degree, especially those numerate based including Scientific, Engineering, Arts etc
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear client-ready materials
High attention to detail and strong organisational skills
Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities
Sound judgement and a strong understanding of risk, compliance, and regulatory expectations







Process

This application shall be open until we reach a cap of applicants, so please apply early. We are hiring for our client across their key global markets. Thereafter once selected, you will undergo our client one day assessment centre four interviews and one group role play. A decision will be made on the day.




Be sure to Follow Us on LinkedIn for updates on this vacancy and others: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cfcorp




Your first step is to apply with your LinkedIn profile and CV. We do not need a covering letter. Due to the volume of applications we receive, we are unfortunately unable to provide individual feedback.







Reward Structure

You will be well compensated for this time and the intern program will last for six weeks. We are running three programs from mid-June, end June and mid-July
Receive a bonus for your time
A permanent offer of employment and part of the Banks’ graduate program
Relocation is provided by the company. Starting salaries will be paid in USD for India $125,000 and up to $150,000 depending on experience
Bonus program of on-target bonus of 50% of base salary and up to 125% for high-achievers
